subject: Project Management
title: SAFe: The Enterprise Framework
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on SAFe: The Enterprise Framework.

hook.text: Agile was born for small teams. Enterprises with thousands of developers needed something bigger.

buildup.text: Dean Leffingwell created SAFe — the Scaled Agile Framework — to bring Agile to large organizations.

discovery.text: SAFe organizes teams into 'Agile Release Trains' that plan and deliver together in 10-week cycles.

twist.text: Agile purists hate SAFe. They call it 'waterfall in disguise' — too much process, too little agility.

climax.text: SAFe is the most widely adopted scaling framework — and the most controversial.

punchline.text: Scaling Agile might mean scaling the thing that kills it.

quiz.question: Why do Agile purists criticize SAFe?
options: They consider it too process-heavy — 'waterfall in disguise'
It only works for software teams
